title: Pretty good earphones, tons of value.
text:
These are pretty good earphones. I can compare them sonically to a number of high-to-low end iems that I've owned though these are only the 2nd pair of Bluetooth completely wireless iems I've owned. So how do these stack up?

Sound Quality: First and foremost the number 1 consideration for me when buying headphones. Well, these sound pretty good. They have a very lush midrange though the high end is a little muted and the low end a little loose. It's enough to know that you aren't paying premium dollars for these headphones but at $50, they feel like a pretty great deal.

Fit: Out of the box these fit me pretty good. Because of that I didn't need to swap out the silicone seals to try to get a better fit. If you do need to adjust though, there are 2 additional sets in the box (small and large with medium being the default).

Comfort: Pretty good once I got them positioned correctly. Maybe it's just me, but I had to look on the box to see which way

title: Leave a lot to be desired
text:
I really wanted to love these, especially having good history with Skull Candy products. Unfortunately I just can't. The right bud cuts in and out pretty bad, even when holding my phone in the open in my hand. The range is horrible, especially if there is anything between the buds and the device connected. The touch buttons are almost unusable with out supporting the bud with another finger. the pressure require to push the button jams the bud into your ear. When they do work properly, they are pretty good though. Sounds quality and volume are more than sufficient, especially for the price point. The charging case is excellent, so much better than my pixel buds. Unfortunately outside of the tether and case my Pixel Buds still beat these in almost every single other way. I feel 3 stars is being generous.

Edit: Upped to 4 star. A reset drastically improved function! The range still isn't what I was hoping for compared to other, older, devices I own. The right bud cutting in an

title: Battery life is not worth it
text:
I was really happy with the size and didn't think the short battery life would be an issue but it really is. I was able to find better quality earphones for a cheaper side that did not have any of the below cons.

Pros:
- small earpiece
- sound quality is great
- not too expensive

Cons:
- around 3-hour battery life
- unable to use the right earphone without turning the left one on
- when only using the left earphone, have run into issues with the base registering that the earphone is back in. This requires me to lift the right headphone from the base and setting it to charge and then doing the same to the left.
- Earphones pick up a lot of background noise and are not user-friendly when picking up calls

title: Best of all I've tried
text:
My mom got me a pair of bluetooth earbuds for Christmas and the right side just didn't connect, so I returned those and got another pair. Those had horrible static in the background of every song. Returned those and just decided to get my trusty SkullCandy. I hadn't tried them first because I saw they only had 3 star reviews. I use their wired earbuds and have only ever used them. I'm so glad I got these.

Positives:
Great sound quality
Great with bass and high pitch
Connects very easily
Long battery life on both buds and case
Small carrying case that's easy to connect to
Buttons to change the song, play, pause, etc (I don't like the touch activated because it's always too sensitive, so I like that these are actual buttons)
Stays in ear well and comes with different sizes to allow proper fit
Small and not super obvious in the ear

Negatives:
Not a very long range connection
Button has to be pushed deep causing some e

title: If looking for independent pairing, don't buy this.
text:
Didn't know this at time of purchase, but these buds don't pair independently. I have cheap $30 buds that do, but don't fit well, so I opted for these. While the sound quality is better than the cheaper pair, I can only use the left bud in mono mode.

The new version buds, Sesh Evo, can pair independently, but this shouldn't be considered a bonus feature. Ear buds have been around long enough that this should be standard on 1st gen devices, not added as an afterthought in later models.

Battery life is also an issue. Whether alone or paired, battery life is just over 3 hours. My aforementioned cheaper buds, Splendid Sound iQute, lasts over 4 hours each bud. I can get a full workshift of sound out of my buds before they die out.

Always loved Skull Candy wired buds and headphones before, and this purchase was my first wireless Skull Candy pair, but very disappointing.

title: Another new favorite set of earbuds? Looks like it.
text:
A while back I fell in love with a set of Panasonic wireless earbuds. (They’re “wireless” but still connected to each other.) My wife loves her AirPods but they didn’t satisfy me (and they're ridiculously expensive), although I remained open to the possibility of using separate earbuds. Then I got the opportunity to try the Soundcore Anker Liberty Air True earbuds and I absolutely fell in love with them at first listen. Of course, I remained open to the possibility that another set of earbuds could knock them off the throne.

Enter Skullcandy Sesh. They were already mostly charged and easily paired with my iPhone. The sound is clear, with rich, velvety bass. Almost too much bass at first, but I adjusted my EQ settings on Spotify and I think they may have broken in over the past few weeks. Listening to music on them is a delight. I primarily listen to podcasts (with the left earbud in only); while the Anker earbuds allow only the use of the right bud by itself, these are compl

title: Good sound quality
text: I actually ordered these for the noise canceling. And once you get the correct size picked out (it comes with 3) they work great. Sound quality is great in my book . Easy to pair with the Skullcandy app and with all the streaming services I have . Battery life is about what is stated but I did notice it goes down if the volume is increased or a movie or music has a lot of loud sounds. But for the price you can’t beat them .

title: Best completely wireless earbuds
text: Love love love these earphones! I missed out when they were on sale before the holidays however I have no regret in paying full price for them. They fit really well with the tips that come on them but there is a smaller and larger one included if you need to switch it out. The battery life of the earphones I could say in active use will last about 3 hours but after about an hour of charging it’s at full charge. The charging case is cute and compact. The case itself holds enough juice to last to the end of the week with regularly charging the earphones. I’ve always loved the skullcandy brand so when I can across this completely wireless I knew I was gonna get a quality product.

title: It is a bit tricky to pair with a device
text: This is a bit difficult to pair because you can easily switch it into mono mode (listen to audio through one earbud) and stereo mode (listen to audio through both earbuds) and not even know it. The instructions don't mention that so you are better off looking up the product on the manufacturer website which breaks things down very well. Pairing is easy once you are familiar with how it works because it doesn't work like most bluetooth devices. Apparently the left earbud is the dominant earbud and it does show up separately on the bluetooth list as a left earbud to pair with instead of the option to pair both. They are small but they do pack a powerful punch with the audio. It does handle noise cancellation well but there isn't an on/off option that I know of. The case charges the earbuds within an hour and the case itself takes about 2 hours to charge. The battery in the earbuds last almost all day.

title: I use them every night
text: These are great! I use them every night when I go to bed, and listen to audio books.  I can’t even hear the air conditioner or the hubby’s snoring 😴

title: Sounds great!
text: These really are quite handy and allow you to interchange the earpiece for comfort. You have to use both at once in order to have volume control, and the blue toothing is different than my other sets of earbuds. The sound is amazing though and truly enjoy the full sound. The price seems suitable for the sound quality.

title: Great bass
text: Bought these for hubby, he loves them! I have used them several times. The sound quality is amazing, the bass is great. Good noise cancellation. Be aware that the battery won't last as long as some cheaper versions due to this, hubby says usually about 4 hrs. We think it's totally worth it. Buy a pair and see for yourself!

title: Great sound at a great price?
text: These earbuds were very easy to set up and use.  The sound was great!  They come with all the standard operational feathers with touch activation.  The only downside was that they did not fit my ears well enough for me to use them when I am running or doing high activity movements without falling out of my ears.

title: Surprisingly nice earbuds!!
text: I didnt expect these buds to be this good. I was really surprised to hear and feel the performance of these headphones.  The battery life is really good. The voice calls sound great. But the thing I was wondering the most was music performance. Wow!! They sound really good!! Not the best out there but the bass is really nice and good sound signature. Also the fit is perfect for my ears.

title: It last 2 weeks off of one charge
text: My daughter got these to connect to her IPHONE because she wanted something that would stay in her ear. She has been an Android user up to now and wanted something she was familiar with before spending a lot of money on IPhone ear pods. Well now, she said she doesn't need the ear pods because these work GREAT! Huge price difference and the sound is amazing. I'm thinking about getting myself a pair.

title: Garbage
text: These were a total disappointment

title: Great for the price
text: Good sound, battery life, fit, and noise isolation for the price. I didn't think I'd ever buy true wireless buds, but couldn't say no at this price point, especially on sale. These are much more convenient to pop in than wired or tethered  headphones. Easily pop out of their case and automatically connect to my phone.

title: Great value, great support
text: These buds sound good considering their low price. User support is impressive -- between the included graphic instructions and Skullcandy's website, I had no trouble pairing them with all my Bluetooth devices.

title: Not happy
text: Disnt last 3 months, maybe used 10times, the left earbud wont charge no matter what I do,and now I have to send them back on my dime?? I guess I'm going to have to buy new ear buds that will last longer than 3 months

title: Being returned 36 hours after being received as a gift
text: My son got these for Christmas (2019) and we are have started a return within 36 hours of him having opened them. One won’t turn on any longer also isn’t charging in the box. I doubted the crappy reviews and I was wrong.

title: Good sound, good comfort, easy to use but lots of problems
text:
First the good. These sound great! I'm definitely not an audiophile but I do enjoy good quality products and my audio is no exception. I can't compare these to high end options but overall the sound quality is completely acceptable with balanced bass and clear highs. They are also quite comfy and I've had no issues with them coming out of the ear unexpectedly.

Neutral: I wish the case had a built in charging cord like some of the competition. Not a necessity but definitely convenient. Additionally, I don't care for micro-USB as I have found it can wear out easily over time. A built-in USB cord would have addressed this as well.

The Bad: Lots of connection issues. Lots of intermittent connection, lots of dropped L/R channel, sometimes some interference noise. Mono mode usually worked out well (using left bud only) but often would drop that, too. Even just bending over at the waist would cut them out if my phone was obscured by my body. Skullcandy warra

title: MAX volume lower than expected.
text: I really like skull candy they were are my go to wired ear buds to go to as for these wireless ones I feel like they need a more higher volume setting. I've tried numerous songs at the highest setting and it sounds like 3/4 of volume compared wired buds as for the bass, skull candy is still rock on.

title: Great value, good performance
text: I bought these as a deal of the day two weeks ago.  For the price, they work very well and are a great value.  I needed these primarily as playback monitor for recording.  I have not yet used for extended music or movie listening.  I find them pretty comfortable compared to apple or other standard wired ear phones.

title: Comfy
text: Bought these because they were on sale more than because I had a specific item in mind. I’m not big on headphones all around as they typically irritate my ears and constantly fall out. These buds fit snugly without feeling awkward and having to position and reposition. They sound nice, they fit nice, and I love the magnetic feature. It snaps them right into place to charge. You know they’re in the case properly the second they are seated due to the magnets. I can also get to the other end of my small 1300 sqft house around corners and all before they have connection issues. I didn’t have to carry my phone around while I was cleaning up at all.

title: Decent but has an issue, Unsure
text: So I was debating whether to get AirPods or give SkullCandy a chance. The 2 year warranty plus being an American company was what drawn me to this product. Unfortunately, I am leaning towards returning these ear pods since the lowest volume it can go to is still too loud- almost to the point it’s hurting my ears drums. If I go below a certain volume on the volume gauge, it just stop producing any sound, and once I slowly slide the volume up, the threshold at which it starts producing sound is too loud for my comfort. I tested a friend’s airpods and with it, I was able to move the volume gauge as low as I wanted to (No this is not an airpods commercial). I even tested the volume with another friend’s Jabra wireless earbuds and the volume I like(obviously the price points for those are triple, quadruple the amount). I’m not sure if the ones I got is a defect but slightly bummed since I was early waiting for these. Decent budget option I guess

title: Comfortable Quality Truly Wireless Headphones
text: This is my first pair of completely wireless headphones and I am pleasantly surprised.  I have bluetooth headphones with the ear pod portion connected and sometimes those seem to pop out of my ears so I was worried these would do the same.  They actually stay in my ear very well and they are quite comfortable.  Adjustments to the song, volume, etc. are done by pushing the headphone itself.  The clarity is pretty good for bluetooth headphones as is the range from my phone.  Others have said it is difficult to hear me during phone calls so they may not be the best for that purpose.  They charge in their case and have a button on the front which shows you their charge level.  Overall these are a great option for anyone looking for an inexpensive pair of bluetooth headphones.

title: Good sound
text: Charges fast, pairs easily, sounds good and clear.

title: Ok ear pods
text: As a whole they are ok gor the price.  Now that I've used and had them for 2months I can give a better review. I get roughly 4hrs of music play. I do need to charge daily

title: Great sound! ❤️
text: Love these!! They sound so awesome!

title: Earbuds with charging case
text: These ear buds has great bass a and they are loud came with a charge box and already charged. They fit perfectly in my ears and no problem with pairing them. These are most certainly the best earbuds I have

title: been trying several brands and while this is the best wireless, My senso's hold a charge longer
text: These fit my ears well but don't hold a charge well. I have a Senso wireless headphones/buds that work better but don't fit nearly as well in my ears.

title: The battery life is not long
text: I am very very disappointed in this product. in less than 5 months the case stop charging. It was very expensive for all of this problem. i was so upset i could not write a review at the time.

title: I drown people out with music
text: I'd been looking at this for months but haven't bought them as I have other obligations. I finally went ahead and got them on sale and woah. The noise cancellation is incredible. I love that they have different size ear cushions and they have quite a few colors to pick from. My family keeps looking at mine, I guess I have a couple of Christmas gifts to buy!

title: They tend to slip out of my small ears but my brother loves them
text:
I bought these for my brother. He is very picky about his headphones especially when it comes to sound quality. He has never been interested in the Apple AirPods because he doesn't like the sound quality. But he loves these. They sound great and block all other noises out. They tend to slip out of my ears but he has no problem with that.

His only complaint is the amount of pressure you have to apply to click the button can be frustrating since they are in your ears.

title: Trash
text: Battery life is awful.  Sound quality as well. And you cant use one at a time. Voice calls only come in on the right side but you cant even answer or talk unless you have both in

title: I just love Skull Candy.
text: Love the comfort. It came with different sizes of ear buds. The sound is amazing. I just had to learn how to adjust them. It paired super easy with my iPhone.

title: One Earbud ceases to work
text:
Really enjoy skullcandy products in the past and this was my first wireless version from them

The charging can be really finnicky, getting it to sit right in the case and sometimes it will just stop charging for no reason

After a couple months though the Left earbud does not work. At first it seemed to be a charging issue but now it will read 100% battery and just shut off seconds after connecting. I used this bud more than the other so I could hear my co-workers, really disappointed in the longevity of this product

title: Love
text: I love these. They fit nicely, and the sound quality is amazing!

title: Manage your expectations, based on the price point
text: These are good, not great, earbuds. They do a decent job at noise cancellation, the sound quality is about high average, and the battery life is about 6 hours, as described. I've had mine for six months and both earbuds are still working, unlike numerous other reviews I've read. They charge quickly, which is a plus since the battery life doesn't last all day. At first, the fit was a bit uncomfortable in my small-ish ears, but I either became accustomed to it or my ears stretched slightly to accommodate. I am a little anxious when moving around that they will fall out. The major downside is they stop receiving if I go too far from the output source, maybe about 30 - 40 ft away.

title: Sound Quality Outside Inconsistent
text:
The sound quality outside is very inconsistent, audio cuts in and out as others have described.
This company has great sound quality with their wired products, was expecting same but didn’t happen.
Need these outside more for dog walking and roller skating

title: Worth it, get em!
text: I'm in love with these. Sound quality is perfect, great noise canceling and picks up my voice perfectly on calls. Comes out crystal clear.

title: Don't buy
text: Wanted these ear bud because the headphones are amazing. Unfortunately these earbuds socks. The battery life only last 2 or 3 hours. Not sure where 10 hours came from. I don't like the fact when your in the phone only the left bud works but when listening to music or watching videos both buds work. I wouldn't buy these again.

title: Crap. Save your money.
text: Good for about two months of only using 2-3 times a week. Wont charge now. Auto connects to my phone when it's not supposed to. Stays connected to my phone even when I take them off and put them in their case to charge, and they just die. Literal garbage. I was so excited for these. Cant use them for jogging or even walking, either they will break connection and get static. Or fall out of your ears. These are so expensive for what you get. Ill still to my $20 JBL bluetooth headphones. Over these.

title: do not easily switch B/T between iphone and mac
text:
Each time I try to use with my mac I have to delete and re-pair.

sound quality is great

battery life is ok, but the "new" sesh evo version is much better

The SkullCandy warranty is great- we lost the left earbud and they had me send in the remaining bud and charger case and then replaced with new- impressive!

title: Bummer subpar headphones
text:
Dies quickly, I bought them for my mother for Christmas but they're stuck in a mono setting which is common issue from what I've researched of others having similar issues..
Which is a bummer cause she did like them.

title: Love skull candy!
text: Sound great and sync without any issues!

title: Work Great
text: My son swears by them. Absolutely loves them.

title: Don’t purchase
text: Probably the worst bluetooth anything I’ve ever purchased. Only works if the phone is like 3 feet from your ears, defeating the purpose, in my opinion. Also, sound quality is poor.

title: disappointed
text: Really disappointed in these, we've been using skullcandy for years and really expected more. the bluetooth is really spotty and very frustrating. The sound quality is okay, but they don't get very loud. Overall definitely wouldn't purchase again

title: You get what you pay for
text: Other than some minor issues getting the buds to charge its great! These won’t blow you away by any means but for the price they are great headphones to carry around in case you need em.

title: Great choice
text: The sound quality is great and the battery life is long

title: Terrible headphones
text: I wanted to love them but it’s so hard when only one side works. Do not buy worst product by skull candy. I’m so annoyed I’m trying to walk and right headphone goes in and out fully charged. Most useless headphones.

title: Go aftermarket for ear adapters
text:
The earbuds themselves are great. The most severe problem I had with them is the poor quality rubber silicone whatever ear adapters that are provided. I liberated a mismatched pair of quality ‘skullcandy’ brand ear rubbers from a pair of long retired corded earbuds to substitute for what was provided.
- One of the provided rubbers got stuck in my ear canal after dozing off within a day of purchase; slightly embarrassing, but be warned.

title: Not good with MacBook Pro- mic doesn't work for video chat
text: Working from home has me doing video chats all day- and my ear holes started to get a little angry after using the headphones that came with my iPhone for extended periods of time. I got these and they work fairly well for listening to music (a little distortion from time to time) but for some reason I can't get the mic to work.  I can use these for a few moments during a call, and then the mic stops working.  Marketing and reviews flood google searches, so useful trouble shooting conversations are hidden and the tech support chat on the skullcandy website had some suggestions that didn't work.

title: Love it
text: My son loves them . He said he can't hear anything other then his music when he wears them . Will definitely be buying more

title: Poor Connection
text: I tried to like these. They are heavy and I feel like they are falling out of my ear constantly. I can also make a call and they work just fine and then make a call a couple minutes later and it will not connect. My phone is in range but it will not connect. Disappointed with the product.

title: Great sound
text: For comfortably and sound great,  u can't tell the difference between my Skulls and daughters apple ipods

title: Looks good sounds okay
text: They look nice they fit well but the sound was not what I was expecting I use Skullcandy plug in headphones and they sound better than these

title: Durable
text: Great quality and have been very durable

title: My son loves them!
text: Bought these for my 14 year old son and he loves them

title: Not a good choice
text:
It doesn’t work all the time ,
Didn’t like it and had to return it

title: Don't buy
text: I bought these due to good reviews. After 2 months the right earbud stoped charging and won't turn on after 3 months. Skullcandy emailed back, since I have a warranty I can pay shipping with tracking and they will investigate! Do not buy these horrible product and customer service!

title: Right ear bud constantly cuts in and out.
text: I should listened to the reviews, the right ear bud is constantly cutting out. Not all the time, but enough of the time where it’s annoying as hell. At one point I couldn’t get the right one to pair at all. Had to delete pairing and pair again. They would be great besides that.

title: Not Impressed
text: I am not sure if I just have high standards, but I would not suggest these to a friend or family member. There has to be a better product out on the market than these. The quality of sound is poor, and I have adjusted the sound settings on my phone multiple times to see if would make a difference. That did not help at all. The battery life is likely the only good thing about this product.

title: Great!
text: Holds a charge for up to 7 hours. The case itself, I'm not sure but I've only had to charge it once a week maybe. Sound is great!

title: Look good fit well
text: Work great charges last a long time great product

title: Low volume
text: They are trash and I love skull candy

title: Amazing quality/ value
text: These are amazing. I would have paid much more for these.

title: Connection issues
text: So annoying…I have to reconnect every time I try to use and I’ll be able to use them successfully for one song before they power down and I have to reconnect again. It’s ridiculous. I’ve yet to actually be able to use these. Waste of money.

title: Happy customer
text: My son loved these. Says they cancel noise better than his AirPods when doing homework.

title: Sound better then AirPods at a fraction of the price and actually stay in your ear!
text: I tried the AirPods but they just would keep falling out and I did not think the sound was that great.  I had been looking for another pair of truly wireless ear buds and decided to give these a try.  They fit in my ear and don't fall out.  Pairing was super easy and the sound quality is great.  I would highly recommend them.

title: Great battery life and quality
text:
I can't really comment on the audio quality as I have significant hearing loss, but the battery life on these wireless earbuds are great. I use them for Podcast primarily and they last about 3 to 4 hours on a single charge. I like how quickly they recharge in the carrying case.

They feel really solid compared to a lot of the other wireless earbuds I've tried.

title: Right ear no longer works after 2 months!!
text: My son wore these earbuds frequently for almost 2 months. The right ear bud recently  stopped working out of the blue. The weird part is, that he usually only wears the left earbud, so the right earbud was rarely used. He says, "that the sound was great, but the battery life is trash". Aside from the obvious,  He would not recommend these headphones based off of the fact that he's had his over the ear JBL headphones for 2 years and they're still going strong. He purchased the red.

title: INSTANT HEADACHE/Bad Sound Quality
text: First off, they didn’t sound the best. The sound crackled and broke up the first time I used them, even after a full charge. Secondly, INSTANT HEADACHE!!! I looked it up and it’s a thing with some people. Heck, if you are going to put Radiation in your ears, between your brain, you have to expect there’s a negative repercussion. One day we may look back and realize it was a completely dumb thing to do but do your thing, I’m going back to wired ear buds. I don’t mind at all. Plus wireless ear pods will just fall out and I’ll lose them or who knows. Some things are meant to never change.

title: Great Buy
text: It’s a perfect set of wireless ear phones!

title: Not worth the money.
text: I always lived skull candies. The battery life is terrible. Only lasts me a few hours. It doesn't stay connected to the docking station so I dont know if the ear buds are actually charging.

title: Okay but some quirks
text: I do like these but they consistently will go out in one ear even if my phone is in my pocket. Good charge life not super great sound cancelling wish the volume went louder.

title: Earbuds fit comfortably.
text: Good right out of the box.  Fit comfortably in both ears and stay securely.  I shook my head while wearing them and they did not fall out.  It will be good to wear while jogging then.  I have not tested for sweat proof, and I have no intention of going to shower or swimming with these on to test whether it's waterproof.  I am glad with my choice.  And I am confident with Skullcandy brand b/c I own a few other Skullcandy headphones as well.

title: Works well, when it works.
text:
Right out of the box, these wouldn't pair with my iMac. Then when it finally did pair, the right earbud remained silent, and the left earbud had horrible sound quality. I looked up tips, tutorials, advice, no solutions worked.

That being said, they paired to my iPhone perfectly. Beautiful sound and connectivity. No issues at all there. But if you're like me, you'll want something that connects to all your devices with that same ease. I ended up just getting the RayCons, which I'm very happy with because none of these issues were present. Nicer charging case as well. The Sesh case is clunky.

title: Nice
text: I received a set if these as a gift and loved them so ordered my mom a set and she loves them as I do.

title: Low battery life
text: Noise quality is good however the battery life is like 3 hrs when it says 10 hrs. I charge it but still they die within 3-4 hrs of using them.

title: They fall off easily...
text: At first I thought they were damaged, because one has a Magnet and the other doesn't, but it's because it automatically lifts so that it automatically connects when you open the lid of the charger, the only thing that I really dislike is, that they fall easily from the ear, they are not for running o exercise, they are more for like being relaxed sitting or laying in bed, but it does has great sound quality!

title: Issues with connectivity and battery life
text: The sound quality was great! However, the battery would die in less than two hours of listening to music. Also the right headphone kept having connection issues and it was audible in the headphone. When talking on the phone, the receiving end said I sounded far away. I have an iPhone 12 so idk if it’s fully compatible with iOS. I could’ve gotten a defective unit too because SkullCandy has always been a good brand in the past.

title: They're not bad
text: I like them, and they do what they're intended to do, however, they slip right out of my ear a lot, and only the left earbud works by itself outside of the case, the right one does not

title: Awesome
text: I bought these as a gift. I got them delivered to my house in less than 24 hours. They fit him great and he loves them. The sound quality is great. He had them turned way up and I could not hear his music as long as the buds were in his ears.

title: Not Worth it
text: Battery is horrible. A little over two hours. No bass at all. I have had these for a week and right side stopped working. Spend a little more money else where. Poor quality by skullcandy. Disappointed!
